body: {"ingredients": {"olive oil": {"unit": "tbsp", "count": 2.0}, "sausage": {"unit": "lb", "count": 1.0}, "eggplant": {"unit": "lb", "count": 1.0}, "red onion": {"unit": "whole", "count": 1.0}, "kosher salt": {"unit": null, "count": null}, "black pepper": {"unit": null, "count": null}, "tomato sauce": {"unit": "oz", "count": 24.0}, "oregano": {"unit": null, "count": null}, "red pepper flakes": {"unit": null, "count": null}, "garlic clove": {"unit": "clove", "count": 2.0}, "dried pasta": {"unit": "lb", "count": 1.0}, "basil": {"unit": null, "count": null}, "pecorino cheese": {"unit": null, "count": null}}, "instructions": ["Heat olive oil in a large skillet or pot over medium heat.", "Add sausage and poke holes sporadically to allow fat and steam to escape.", "Cook, turning occasionally, until well browned and cooked through, 10\u201312 minutes.", "Remove sausage and set aside, leaving the fat in the pan.", "Add the eggplant and onion; season with salt and pepper.", "Cook, stirring occasionally, until eggplant is nicely browned and tender and onions start to fry at the edges, 10\u201315 minutes, adding more olive oil as needed.", "Add tomato sauce and season again with salt and pepper.", "Add about 2 cups water (rinse the jar or can to get all the bits) along with oregano and/or red pepper flakes.", "Bring to a simmer, occasionally crushing eggplant to encourage it to break down.", "Add the garlic and cook until the sauce thickens and eggplant is totally softened, 10\u201312 minutes.", "Meanwhile, cook pasta in a large pot of salted boiling water until just al dente.", "Using a slotted spoon, transfer pasta to the sauce and cook together until pasta is fully cooked, another few minutes.", "Divide pasta among bowls; top with torn basil and grated Pecorino or Parmesan.", "Serve sausage on the side with the pasta and, if you like, a simple salad."]}
